channel.title: Data Minimization, Fairness and Accountability on Veronica Profile
channel.description: Recent content in Data Minimization, Fairness and Accountability on Veronica Profile

item.title: Anonymous yet Tracked … Anonymity Boldly Promised but Quietly Broken!
item.description: The Login Hoax: At the completion of a course, a survey is set up, and students must access a URL in their web browser, log in with their username and password to provide responses for course evaluation. Responses are said to be treated as anonymous. A neat administrative paradox, as students are promised anonymity only after completing a login process which makes them identifiable. The login requirement works against data privacy and transparency causing informed consent failure due to misleading information. This failure results in students’ mistrust of the survey process and data privacy irregularities because they do not understand why their identity or login details are needed from the onset of the survey. Authenticated anonymity weakens the claim of anonymity and undermines several GDPR principles. The core idea of the GDPR principle is to collect less, explain more, and protect everything. In practice, this means collect only the data that is necessary, including any information that can identify an individual; explain clearly what data will be collected, why it is needed, and how it will be used; and protect or safeguard any data that is retained, stored, or processed.
